title: selenipedium × grande var. macrochilum
intro: Selenipedium × grande var. macrochilum, also known as the 'Orchid of the Andes', is a hybrid orchid species derived from the cross between Selenipedium palmifolium and Selenipedium ishingeri. This plant contains compounds like selenipine and seleninine, which exhibit potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Selenipedium × grande var. macrochilum are largely anecdotal, with reported uses including treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea that is said to offer relief from various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
